recommendations: Continue enforcing all locked decisions and non-negotiable invariants strictly
Maintain and update phase status and docsVerified flags accurately in milestone metadata
Run all drift and calibration checks (vision, docs, legibility, standards, evaluator, adversarial, inference) before milestone completion
Keep harness rules and remediation steps synchronized with runtime behavior and documentation
Ensure all new behavior includes success-path and failure-path tests
Maintain machine-checked feature catalog and evidence for all implemented capabilities
Preserve repository legibility and team standards with automated drift gates and remediation reporting
Keep evaluator calibration and adversarial taxonomy reports current and enforce blocking on critical findings
Use inference drift reports and enforce with gate mode as needed to prevent drift
Update runbook and operational documentation with each phase progress and verify with drift checks

ruleHints.hint: Ensure the workflow system prompt is non-empty and includes explicit role, constraints, and verification posture.
resolutionSteps: Set a non-empty workflow.systemPrompt with clear instructions.
Include explicit role definitions, constraints, and verification intent in the prompt.
Reject deployment or execution if the system prompt is blank.

ruleHints.hint: Add explicit constraint language using MUST, NEVER, DO NOT, or ONLY to the workflow system prompt.
resolutionSteps: Incorporate non-negotiable guardrail language in the system prompt.
Document tool and data boundaries clearly within the prompt.

ruleHints.hint: Include verification and acceptance language such as PASS, FAIL, VERIFY, or ACCEPTANCE in the system prompt.
resolutionSteps: Define explicit verification expectations in the system prompt.
Add PASS/FAIL acceptance criteria for workflow outputs.
Ensure verification language is clear and actionable.

ruleHints.hint: For commit tool nodes, ensure a matching propose phaseGroup node exists to enable preview and verification.
resolutionSteps: Add a tool node with sideEffectMode='propose' and matching phaseGroup before commit nodes.
Keep commit nodes with sideEffectMode='commit' and the same phaseGroup.
Emit preview artifact hash before executing commit actions.

ruleHints.hint: Specify timeoutMs and retryLimit for each node to manage execution time and retries.
resolutionSteps: Add timeoutMs and retryLimit properties to all nodes.
Implement escalation to human operators after retry budget exhaustion.

ruleHints.hint: Define a valid memoryPolicy with retrievalMode, maxContextItems, writebackEnabled, and piiRetention settings.
resolutionSteps: Set memoryPolicy.maxContextItems to an integer between 1 and 200.
Explicitly configure memoryPolicy.writebackEnabled and piiRetention values.
Validate memoryPolicy against schema before deployment.

suggestions.attack: An attacker crafts user input that attempts to override or neutralize system-level prompts or constraints, causing the system to ignore security policies.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Strict parsing and sanitization of user inputs to detect and block attempts to override system prompts; enforce immutable system prompt constraints.

suggestions.attack: A workflow node calls a tool with overly broad or wildcard permissions (e.g., '*'), allowing access to unauthorized resources or data.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Enforce strict permission scopes on all tool calls; disallow wildcard or unscoped permissions; require explicit least privilege declarations.

suggestions.attack: Workflow objective or system prompt lacks explicit denial of cross-tenant data access, allowing implicit or indirect access to other tenants' data.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Require explicit tenant boundary language denying cross-tenant access; validate workflow text for ambiguous or permissive language.

suggestions.attack: An attacker injects malicious or misleading data into the memory context, causing the system to make incorrect decisions or leak sensitive data.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Implement memory context validation and sanitization; monitor for anomalous or inconsistent memory entries; restrict writeback permissions.

suggestions.attack: User input includes embedded code or commands that the system executes unintentionally, leading to arbitrary code execution or data exfiltration.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Escape and sanitize all user inputs; disable or tightly control code execution features; use safe interpreters or sandboxes.

suggestions.attack: A workflow node gains access to tools or APIs beyond its intended scope by exploiting misconfigured permission scopes.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Enforce per-node tool permission scoping; audit and restrict tool access; implement runtime permission checks.

suggestions.attack: Data from one tenant is leaked to another tenant via shared caches, logs, or temporary storage.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Isolate tenant data in all shared resources; implement strict access controls and data sanitization on shared components.

suggestions.attack: Memory policies allow retention of personally identifiable information (PII) without proper redaction, enabling privacy violations.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Enforce strict PII retention policies; require redaction or anonymization of sensitive data in memory; audit memory writeback settings.
